title: Phyllorkis pendula
taxonomy: Phyllorkis pendula is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. It is a member of the genus Phyllorkis, which is a group of orchids. Phyllorkis pendula is further classified as a member of the subtribe Malaxideae, a group of terrestrial orchids. The plant is native to the Himalayas and parts of Asia, and is characterized by its pendant flowers and strap-like leaves. Phyllorkis pendula is a relatively rare and exotic orchid species, often grown by orchid enthusiasts for its unique and delicate appearance.
